Bertinelli and Van Halen were married from 1981 to 2007 and had one child together, Wolfgang Van Halen. Then, she was married to Vitale from 2011 to 2022. Following the end of that marriage, she confirmed she was dating writer Mike Goodnough in April 2024. But the two called it quits in November 2024, People reported at the time.
Months later, she penned a lengthy post on Instagram “about mistakes I’ve made in relationships.” She also wrote in the now-deleted post that she “fumbled the last true good man I met.”
During her recent sit-down with People, she reflected on her relationship history.

“Two divorces behind me, I felt like a failure. I didn’t know what love actually meant, really, real true (love),” she shared. “Ed and I had it for glimpses, but I really never really experienced it for any length of time. And I still haven’t. You know, Ed was the closest I’ve ever come to feeling true true love.”

She said she needs a partner who makes her feel safe. In turn, she would like “someone to feel emotionally safe with me, like they can open up and be vulnerable with me because I’ll protect that and I want to feel protected. And I haven’t experienced that either where my vulnerability has been respected and held on to.”
Bertinelli confirmed at the time that she is open to being in another relationship. But if it doesn’t happen, she is content.
“I mean, love, when it’s right, can be magical. And one day I might get that, but I also have to be OK with one day not getting it. So that’s where I’m at. I love my life right now,” she said.

title: Garcelle Beauvais Speaks Out on Fallout From Her ‘RHOBH’ Exit: ‘A Line Was Drawn for Me’
summary: Garcelle Beauvais spoke on TODAY and in her Audible Originals memoir “Protecting My Peace at All Costs” about why she left “Real Housewives of Beverly House.”
story: Garcelle Beauvais is shedding light on her decision to step away from “The Real Housewives of Beverly Hills” in 2025.
The actor, who first joined the Bravo reality series in Season 10, exited the show after filming Season 14.
During the show’s reunion special, Garcelle walked off the set and refused to take a photo with her co-stars, prompting many viewers to wonder what had gone on behind the scenes.
While appearing May 7 on the 3rd Hour of TODAY, Garcelle offered insight into her decision to leave the show.
“A line was drawn for me that I no longer wanted to cross. I felt like my spirit could no longer do it. (It was) just drama for the sake of drama,” she told NBC’s Savannah Sellers.
It’s a topic Garcelle also discusses in her new Audible Original memoir, “Protecting My Peace ... at All Costs,” as she explains how she felt alone while working with the cast.

“I realized something I couldn’t unsee. I didn’t have an ally. Not one. Not a single friend sitting on that stage with me. No one to lock eyes with, no one to lean toward. No one saying, ‘I see you,’” she wrote. “The same way I walked in is how I walked out. Alone. I didn’t wanna fight anymore. I didn’t wanna be a grown a-- woman, a grandmother, going after other women.”
During her 3rd Hour of TODAY interview, Garcelle reiterated this sentiment.

“And like I said (in my book), I felt like there wasn’t anybody there anymore that I connected with. And it was just time. I mean, ‘Housewives’ was never my last stop. I’ve done a lot of things prior to that and hope to do more afterward. But it was just time for me. It really was,” she said.

In her memoir, Garcelle discusses the drama that went on behind the scenes at the Season 14 reunion, including an incident in 2022 when Garcelle’s new book at the time was put in the garbage by her cast mates.

“At the reunion, I found out that there was a group chat that I was never a part of. That’s how my memoir ended up in the trash, posted on social media. And when it all came out, some of the women laughed, like it was a joke about recycling. How do you feel a part of something when there’s a group chat you didn’t even know existed? I thought about leaving then. I thought about leaving after every reunion, if I’m honest,” she wrote.
Going into the reunion, Garcelle intended to take a cast photo, but she changed her mind while filming, saying she felt “a shift” with her co-stars.

“Andy stopped by my dressing room before taping as he always does. He said, ‘How are you feeling?’ I said, ‘Good.’ He said, ‘Really?’ In a way that made me feel like, ‘Oh, s---. Who’s coming for me?’” she wrote.

Garcelle was one of two stars who joined the show’s cast in Season 10. Over time, she developed a friendship with the other new face, Sutton Brown (aka Sutton Stracke). Their bond crumbled amid Garcelle’s exit from the show.
While appearing on “Watch What Happens Live” in October 2025, Garcelle said her friendship with Sutton was over and explained how she believed that Sutton didn’t support her during the reunion.
“Not only she didn’t have my back, she also when she called me, never took accountability for it and that hurt my feelings a lot,” she said.
In the “RHOBH” reunion this year, Sutton expressed hope that she and Garcelle could one day “reconcile or at least talk.”

In response to Garcelle’s claims that Sutton didn’t have her back, Sutton said: “I don’t know why she thinks that, to be honest. I would have done anything for her and (her sons). Anything — and she knows that.”

title: Billie Eilish and Nat Wolff Made Their Red Carpet Debut. Inside Their Relationship History
summary: Billie Eilish and Nat Wolff are red carpet official. Here’s the history of their relationship, how they met and how long they’ve been linked.
story: Billie Eilish and Nat Wolff are red carpet official.
The duo posed together on the red carpet at the premiere of Eilish’s new concert movie, “Billie Eilish — Hit Me Hard and Soft: The Tour (Live in 3D),” on May 6.

In one photo, the “Birds of a Feather” singer embraced Wolff, an actor and singer who has appeared in several films, including “The Fault in Our Stars” and “Paper Towns.” Wolff got his start in the Nickelodeon show “The Naked Brothers Band,” a mockumentary about his life with his brother.

Eilish, 24, was dressed in a green polo shirt layered over a white blouse and a black pleated skirt. She accessorized with red and blue knee-high socks and silver heels. The musician’s beau, 28, opted for a brown suit and a white shirt.
The two have been linked romantically since last year, with photos published in June 2025 of the two kissing in Venice, Italy.
Most recently, the couple attended the 2026 Grammy Awards together.
Before they were publicly linked, Eilish and Wolff co-starred in the music video for her song “Chihiro” in 2024.
Speaking to Vogue in 2024, Wolff offered the following description of Eilish.

“She’s one of those people where almost everything she does, she does better than everyone else. She’s like, ‘Come see me ride my horse,’ and the teacher says, ‘You know, if she put all her energy into it, she could go to the Olympics,’” he said.
Wolff and his brother, Alex Wolff, served as an opening act for Eilish during her “Hit Me Hard and Soft” tour from 2024 to 2025.

The brothers, in a 2026 interview with Interview magazine, said they learned from Eilish “how to be a world class artist with grace,” all while making fans “feel so special and beloved,” during the tour.
Per Vogue, the two musicians first met at the Academy Museum Gala in Los Angeles and bonded over their shared experience with Tourette syndrome.
While speaking about Eilish, Wolff told Vogue, “You just have to kind of submit to the fact that she’s going to be better than everyone else at everything.”

title: Stanley Tucci Reveals His New TJ Maxx Ad and Opens Up About His Style Evolution
summary: Stanley Tucci opens up about his latest collaboration with TJ Maxx, "The Devil Wears Prada 2" and his favorite fashion purchase of all time.
story: There’s no question that both on-screen and off, Stanley Tucci is a style icon.

Outfitted in his signature glasses and impeccable attire, Tucci is as much known for his dapper fashion as he is for his impressive career, which includes accolades across film, television and Broadway, not to mention a collection of best-selling culinary books.

But it’s style that’s front and center as Tucci returns to the big screen to reprise his role as Nigel Kipling, art director for the fictional magazine Runway in “The Devil Wears Prada 2,” a sequel that’s been 20 years in the making.
“I like the fact that the movie wasn’t made immediately afterwards,” Tucci tells TODAY.com during a phone interview.

“I like the fact that it’s 20 years later, that these people are older and more mature. The world circumstances that we find ourselves in now and the circumstances particularly concerning journalism and AI, the internet... all these changes that are happening now so fast, I think makes it quite an interesting film.”
A lot has changed since the first “Devil Wears Prada” was released back in 2006, including Tucci’s personal style.

“I always liked fashion,” Tucci says. “Before the first film, I always would dress nicely, I always wore a jacket and all that stuff. But after the first film, I became more and more interested in it.”

Citing legendary costume designer Patricia Field as the person who was “instrumental” in helping him unlock the true potential of men’s clothing, Tucci says filming “The Devil Wears Prada” took his fashion game to the next level.
“It opened my mind and that was great,” says Tucci.

Tucci is parlaying that love of fashion into a new ad with TJ Maxx that riffs on the already-classic line from the sequel in which Nigel drolly sniffs, “Look what TJ Maxx dragged in” upon Andy’s (Anne Hathaway) return to the glossy magazine.
“(TJ Maxx) came and they said, ‘Would you be interested in doing an ad?’” Tucci says of the collab. “And the concept was so funny that there was no way I was not going to do it.”
The clever commercial finds Tucci offering a tongue-in-cheek mea culpa for his Nigel’s withering comment saying, “Do you really think that I wrote those words? I’m just a tragically handsome actor.”
He then goes on to admit that far from being like his on-screen counterpart, he’s actually a “Maxxinisto” and, thanks to TJ Maxx, a “very well-dressed” one at that.
“While certain fictional characters may have spoken hastily, I personally have developed a taste for elevated quality without the unnecessary drama of elevated prices,” he sums up in the ad.

While Tucci and Nigel are very different in real life, Tucci clearly shares his character’s love of fine fashion, which begs the question: Is there anything in Tucci’s closet that he considers a serious shopping find?
“I have this jacket. It’s a black leather jacket that is a bit longer,” he says of his score. “It’s really thick and it’s really old.”
According to Tucci, he discovered the one-of-a-kind vintage leather coat in a thrift shop.
“As it turns out, it’s a Chicago policeman’s jacket from the 1940s and it’s made out of horse hide. That’s why it’s so incredibly thick,” explains Tucci.

“I have it in my closet. I seldom wear it, but it’s like, to me, one of the coolest looking ... the leather’s all weathered and messed up, but I can’t get rid of it,” he adds. “I almost want to frame it. You can still see the little shadow or imprint of where the badge was.”
And beyond their mutual love of fashion, Tucci does have some very Nigel-like thoughts on fashion faux pas, including a disdain for athleisure.
“Wear athletic wear when you’re working out – no other time,” Tucci told GQ last month at the premiere of “The Devil Wears Prada 2.”
And he stands by the advice while talking with TODAY.com.
“It’s fine if you’ve just exercised and you have to run to the shop to get something. But, otherwise, no,” says Tucci of the trend of wearing workout clothes in place of more traditional daily wear.
So, is it fair to say that when it comes to good style, fashion wins over comfort?
“Without question,” says Tucci. “It’s not even a question.”

title: Bette Midler, Sarah Jessica Parker and Kathy Najimy to Run Amok in ‘Hocus Pocus 3’
summary: Witches Winifred, Sarah and Mary will again bring chaos and mischief to the world in the upcoming “Hocus Pocus 3.”Bette Midler, Sarah Jessica Parker and Kathy Najimy will reprise their roles as the Sanderson Sisters in the movie, which is in early development at Disney Live Action Studios, TODAY.com has learned.
story: Witches Winifred, Sarah and Mary will again bring chaos and mischief to the world in the upcoming “Hocus Pocus 3.”

Bette Midler, Sarah Jessica Parker and Kathy Najimy will reprise their roles as the Sanderson Sisters in the movie, which is in early development at Disney Live Action Studios, TODAY.com has learned. Deadline first reported the news of the third installment and the possibility that the film will have a theatrical distribution following “Hocus Pocus 2.” The 2022 sequel, also starring Midler, Parker and Najimy, was released on Disney+.

The sisters first wreaked havoc on Salem residents in the original 1993 flick, which has since become a beloved Halloween classic. The movie followed a teenager who accidentally awakened and freed a coven of evil witches who lay dormant in an abandoned house. Nearly 30 years later, another group of naive teenagers brought the witches to life once more.
Fans were delighted to see the trio together again in 2022, and last year the stars gave them hope that another installment was on the way.
In July 2025, Parker appeared on “Watch What Happens Live with Andy Cohen” and revealed that the cast was in talks for “Hocus Pocus 3.”
“No more developments other than we would like to do it,” she said when responding to a fan’s question about the threequel’s status. “We’ve been having some conversations.”

Then a few months later in October 2025, just in time for Halloween, Midler also stopped by Andy Cohen’s talk show and discussed a third film, confirming that a script has already been written thanks to the success of the sequel.
She said “Hocus Pocus 2” amassed “billions of watches” on the streaming platform. “Billions, I’d never heard that number before,” she added.

When asked about the next movie, she said, “They sent a script and a lot of it was brilliant. So I got very excited. And now we’re sort of, like, trying to figure out what it is and where it’s going to be and how much it’s going to cost.”
She said “all those logistical things” were still being decided.

As for Najimy, she gave fans hope that they will see “Hocus Pocus 3” soon when she posted a picture of her Halloween costume on Instagram in October 2025. Najimy dressed as a witch in an ensemble that Mary would surely approve of, donning her twisted wig.
In the comments, the Disney+ official account replied, “Happy Halloween to our Mary Sanderson.”
Midler, Parker and Najimy have yet to comment on the latest news about their next team up.

title: What Jane Fonda and Her ‘Favorite Ex-Husband’ Ted Turner Said About Each Other
summary: Jane Fonda and Ted Turner were married for a decade, sharing words of encouragement, love and support over the years.
story: Jane Fonda and Ted Turner were one of Hollywood’s hottest couples in the 1990s, but the two grew even closer after they divorced.
Fonda, 88, often jokingly referred to the billionaire media mogul and CNN founder, who died May 6 at age 87 following a 2018 diagnosis of Lewy body dementia, as her “favorite ex-husband.”
Shortly after reports of his death, Fonda penned an emotional tribute to Turner of her “immediate thoughts.”

“(Ted) swept into my life, a gloriously handsome, deeply romantic, swashbuckling pirate and I’ve never been the same,” she began in a lengthy Instagram post that included a picture of the former couple embracing. “He needed me. No one had ever let me know they needed me, and this wasn’t your average human being that needed me, this was the creator of CNN, and Turner Classic Movies, who had won the America’s Cup as the world’s greatest sailor. He had a big life, a brilliant mind and a soaring sense of humor.”
Fonda continued to gush about the late mogul, adding that he took care of her and made her believe in herself.

“He gave me confidence. I think I did the same for him, but that’s what women are raised to do. Men like Ted aren’t supposed to express need and vulnerability. That was Ted’s greatest strength, I believe,” she said.
She added that he also taught her about nature, wildlife, business and other subjects thanks to his wide range of knowledge.
Fonda then complimented Turner for being ambitious, noting he was the most competitive person she knew besides Katharine Hepburn.
“Ted was challenging, but I’ve always been up for a challenge, and with Ted it was almost always worth it,” Fonda wrote.
Her tribute continued in the comments. She said she deeply loved Turner and can picture him in heaven surrounded by wildlife. She also mentioned the love she has for his five children.
She concluded, “Rest in Peace, dearest Ted. You are loved and you will be remembered.”
The unlikely couple, who came from opposite sides of the political aisle, were married from 1991 to 2001.

Fonda, an outspoken liberal activist who grew up the daughter of Hollywood legend Henry Fonda, routinely praised Turner, an Atlanta-based businessman who liked to hunt, in interviews for decades after their split.
“He’s outrageous and hysterically funny,” she told People about her ex in 2023.
Fonda, who, like Turner, had been married twice before, also wrote lovingly of the good times the couple shared in her 2005 memoir, “My Life So Far.”

“There were times when something would set us to laughing so hard we’d sink to the floor, like the night when our guffaws collapsed us at the foot of the ‘Gone With the Wind’ staircase at his plantation and we had to crawl up to bed on hands and knees,” she wrote.
Read on to learn more about what Fonda and Turner have said about each other over the years.
Fonda Was Attracted to Turner’s Energy
Shortly after she and Turner began dating in 1990, Fonda told People she was attracted to the maverick businessman's energy.
“Ted can keep up with me,” she said. “He’s a very, very funny, lovable, complicated person.”
The couple wed at Turner’s 8,100-acre plantation, Avalon, in Capps, Florida, according to People.
They split a decade later, in part, because of Turner’s infidelity, Fonda explained in her memoir.
Turner Recalled Crying After Fonda Left Him
In his own 2008 memoir, “Call Me Ted,” Turner recalled the couple's whirlwind courtship, which began in 1990 when Turner asked Fonda for a date after learning of her divorce from activist Tom Hayden.
Fonda declined, but Turner kept insisting. Six months later, sparks flew when the pair finally met up. They married the following year.
Turner also wrote about the devastation he experienced after Fonda left him.
Returning to his Montana ranch for the first time after their split, Turner saw Fonda had removed her belongings.
“Our closets faced each other’s, and when I saw her empty space I sat down on the floor between them and cried,” he wrote.
Turner Called Fonda the Love of His Life
In 2012, CNN’s Piers Morgan asked Turner if Fonda was the “great love of his life.”
“Probably,” Turner responded.
After more prompting from Morgan, Turner revealed he’d never gotten over losing Fonda and he knew he never would.
“When you love somebody and you really love them, you never stop loving them no matter how hard you try, you can’t stop,” Turner explained.
“There’s nothing wrong with that. That’s good,” he added.
In Fonda’s 2018 documentary “Jane Fonda in Five Acts,” Turner described their separation as “very difficult,” adding, “I thought it was a mistake.”
“I’ve survived and so has she, but I feel like I was happier when I was with her than subsequently,” he said.
Fonda Called Turner ‘Sexy’ and ‘Brilliant’ Years After Their Split
In that same documentary, Fonda recalled the painful end of her and Turner’s marriage.
“Ted was a genius... but he was also gorgeous. I mean, he was really a handsome man, and very, very sexy,” Fonda said, sharing that she was attracted to his “wild brilliance.”
But she also decided to give up her career while she was with him and eventually felt like she couldn't be her full authentic self.

“We had a wonderful 10 years, but I had to hide a part of me in order to please him,” she said, noting there was a voice in her head telling her, “‘If you stay, you will never be authentic, you will never be able to be whole.’”
Fonda Spoke Lovingly of Turner Months Before His Death
Months before Turner died, Fonda sang his praises when she spoke at the Georgia Campaign for Adolescent Power and Potential (GCAPP) EmPower Party and Gala in Atlanta on Nov. 13, 2025.
Fonda acknowledged how Turner’s contributions helped her establish the organization in 1995, noting it, “never would have happened if it wasn’t for Ted,” People reported at the time.

“It was a very challenging time in Georgia. Had I not been with Ted, this was something, had he not stood by me with his love and support, we never would have survived,” Fonda said of the businessman, whose public appearances declined after his 2018 diagnosis.
“Ted’s not here,” she added, “but he is here in my heart and I know he is here in a lot of our hearts.”

title: Hayden Panettiere Publicly Comes Out as Bisexual
summary: Hayden Panettiere opens up about her sexuality, revealing publicly for the first time that she is bisexual and why she waited to share the news at 36.
story: Hayden Panettiere is publicly discussing her sexuality for the first time, revealing she is bisexual and started dating women “at a very, very young age.”

The 36-year-old actor opens up about her personal life in her upcoming memoir, “This Is Me: A Reckoning,” out May 19. In the book, the “Heroes” alum shares details about her past relationships, including with actor Milo Ventimiglia and her former fiancé Wladimir Klitschko, while also addressing her sexuality.
During an interview with Us Weekly published May 6, Panettiere was asked about coming out in the book and her attraction to women.

“That’s something about me I was never able to share with the world, because it was just never the right time,” she said about waiting to publicly label her sexuality. “It was either I was too young, and I was being forced to be perfect at all times. I was not encouraged to just be myself.”

She continued, “Then came the period where it felt like people coming out, especially women, saying that they were bisexual or liked girls, was a fad. I was afraid that if I was honest, it was going to be like me jumping on the bandwagon.”
The actor recalled struggling to find the words to express how she felt.
“It’s sad I had to wait until I was 36 years old to share that part of me, but better late than never, right?” she said.
With her book release coming up soon, Panettiere feels free to embrace all sides of herself.
She added, “I’m comfortable to confidently say that yes, I am bisexual. I said it! This is the first time I got to say it out loud.”
Panettiere told the magazine she has never been in love with a woman. As a celebrity, she found it “scary” to date women in the past.

“It was scary, though, because there were paparazzi always waiting for me outside, to follow me everywhere. I had very little privacy,” she explained. “I have dated women. I was much more into women even as a child than I was men. I have explored it, but because I hadn’t shared this with anybody, I didn’t really have the courage to throw myself fully emotionally into it.”
She said she feared falling in love because she didn’t want to have to hide that relationship from the world.

Speaking more about her decision to come out, Panettiere said it took her two years to write her memoir. During that time, stories about her sexuality came up and she realized she wanted to be “brutally honest.” But, she was still worried.
“And once again, it was always the fear of not being perfect, and what my team was going to think about it, what the public’s opinion was going to be about it,” she told Us Weekly.
She also said she began dating women “at a very, very young age.”
However, all her public relationships were with men. She was engaged to Klitschko, a Ukrainian former professional boxer, from 2013 to 2018. The two share 11-year-old daughter Kaya.
Panettiere has spoken candidly in the past about her struggles and how her life mirrored the alcoholism and postpartum depression storylines her character experienced on “Nashville.”
But in her latest interview, she confirmed that is in a better place and has an “incredible” relationship with her ex and daughter.

“I’m so grateful for Wlad. We’re very close, and have had a deep friendship, along with a relationship, since I met him when I was 19. So to see that we still have this bond and the ability to co-parent and show our daughter (that) even though we’re not together, doesn’t mean that there’s a lack of love here, and it doesn’t mean that you’re unsafe,” she shared.

Praising Kaya, she gushed, “And my daughter, she’s just an incredible gift. She’s otherworldly and so kind and so fierce and surrounded by good people, and it’s incredible for me to be able to watch her thrive in areas that I struggled with growing up. She’s just the best thing, all the best parts of me and Wlad.”

title: The Best Dressed Stars of the 2026 Met Gala
summary: Who were the best dressed celebrities from the 2026 Met Gala? Two fashion experts shared their picks, including Connor Storrie, Rihanna and Eileen Gu.
story: The 2026 Met Gala may be over, but some of the best dressed A-list celebrities now linger in fashion lovers’ minds.

The gala is held every year to raise funds for the Metropolitan Museum of Art’s Costume Institute. This year’s gala theme was inspired by the institute’s spring exhibition: “Costume Art.” The gala’s dress code? The enticing “Fashion is Art.”
Revisit our recap of the 2026 Met Gala

“Why’d They Wear That?” host and QVC ambassador Stacy London joins Julie Matos, a New York City-based celebrity stylist, and Maggie Gillette, a fashion expert and partner at the Curio, to break down some of their favorite looks from the 2026 Met Gala.
Chase Infiniti
Chase Infiniti’s colorful Thom Browne gown, which was inspired by Alexandros of Antioch’s ancient Greek marble sculpture Venus de Milo, was one of Stacy London’s favorite looks for the night.
“Chase proves that color is a serious trend. Not just one color … all the colors! She is truly dressing for joy,” the TV personality tells TODAY.com.
Grace Gummer
London also gave top marks to “Love Story” star Grace Gummer’s golden off-the-shoulder gown by designer Gabriela Hearst.
“I like Grace’s look because it’s the less obvious body sculpture look. It blends more fashion in with art form rather than fashion reproducing art,” says London.
Gabrielle Union
Another hit in London’s book? Gabrielle Union’s sheer, body-hugging Michael Kors gown embellished with a whopping 170,000 crystals.
Union topped off the look with a majestic crown hairstyle and Tiffany & Co. necklace.
“Mesh sparkle and more sheer looks modern in a simple but dramatic silhouette. Also, HELLO body con!” says London.
Rihanna
Rihanna arrived fashionably late (again!) to this year’s gala on the arm of her longtime love, rapper A$AP Rocky.
The “Umbrella” singer’s wild gold and silver metallic look from Maison Margiela, combined with large pieces from Briony Raymond and Dyne jewelry, gets top marks from Matos.

“Rihanna closed the night with absolute authority,” Matos tells TODAY.com. “The sculptural, high-shine gown, finished with dramatic volume and a sweeping train, felt both controlled and expansive, less an outfit, more of a statement.”
Adut Akech
Pregnant model Adut Akech, who turned to designer Thom Browne for an elegant expectant look, is at the top of Gillette’s list.
“Perhaps the chicest maternity look I’ve ever seen,” Gillette tells TODAY.com. “I love the way that Thom Browne created a dress that was at once structured and organic feeling.
“The ring of delicate flowers circling Akech’s stomach perfectly highlighted the pregnant body while celebrating the creativity inherent in bringing new life to the world.”
Connor Storrie
“Heated Rivalry” star Connor Storrie’s custom Yves Saint Laurent look also made London and Gillette’s lists.
“This look was really well balanced. That suit was impeccable,” Gillette explains.

“When I first saw the outfit, Storrie had the jacket on and I thought that he just had a scarf as a piece of ‘flair.’ But later it became apparent it was a beautiful top which he accented with a pin for a pop of color.”
Adds London, “I just love Connor’s arms in this look.”
Naomi Osaka

Tennis champ Naomi Osaka’s dramatic white custom two-piece set from Robert Wun won enthusiastic raves from Matos, who says the look “felt highly detailed and almost cinematic, with layered textures and intricate construction that read as a complete work of art.”
Janelle Monae
Musician Janelle Monae’s wild Christian Siriano gown combined electrical cables and circuit board gadgetry with green moss and butterflies for a commentary on nature and technology.
Matos, for one, loved it.

“Janelle Monae delivered a conceptual Siriano look, blending structured tailoring with both organic and technical details,” she tells TODAY.com. “The wires, texture, and sculptural detail gave it a futuristic, almost AI-driven quality.”
Tory Burch
While several stars showed up to the gala wearing Tory Burch designs, Burch herself opted for a sophisticated vintage Mugler gown.

“This dress showed that a quiet look can have a presence,” Gillette says. “The gown fit like a glove and the styling was absolute perfection. And when she turned-that little capelet with the extra embellishment just put the look over the top for me.”
Hailey Bieber
Model Hailey Bieber’s gold and blue Saint Laurent gown was a win in London and Gillette’s books.
“While we saw a lot of this literal sculpture bodice, I favored the less literal ones, but this is the exception. Beautiful color,” London tells TODAY.com.
Explains Gillette, “Saint Laurent was known for his multiple references to and collaborations with artists, and the dress that Hailey Bieber was modeled after one of them."

“The gold bodice was a replica of the ones that Saint Laurent created with sculptor artist Claude Lalanne when they made ‘body casts’ of the model Veruschka and it captured the intersection of art and fashion beautifully," adds Gillette.
Emma Chamberlain
Matos and London both flipped for influencer Emma Chamberlain’s custom Mugler gown, which was hand-painted by artist and designer Anna Deller-Yee.

Chamberlain, says Matos, “wore a painted, body-contouring Mugler look that treated the body as canvas. The sculptural lines and trompe l’oeil effect kept it sharp and visually striking, while still feeling controlled.”
Adds London, “I love the way this feels like fashion and art combined. The colors are divine.”
Bad Bunny
Matos also applauded rapper Bad Bunny for boldly showing up in a Zara look combined with prosthetics and make-up to make him look like a much older man.

“Bad Bunny wore a tailored Zara look paired with a full prosthetic transformation, aging himself decades forward. The styling turned the look into a statement on time and identity, pushing it beyond fashion into performance,” she says.
Eileen Gu
Olympic skier and model Eileen Gu’s playful Iris Van Herpen gown was a hit with Gillette.
“Whimsy and joy are so underrated these days and I love that Gu and Van Herpen brought that to the Met with this look,” she explains.
“Of course Van Herpen is also an artist and a meticulous technical genius so while we all see the pretty end result that dress reportedly has 15,000 glass bubbles that took 2,500 hours to create.”
Zoë Kravitz
Zoë Kravitz’s lacy black Saint Laurent dress made Gillette and Matos' lists, with Gillette citing the elegance of the look.
“While this structure of the dress was very traditional, the use of a sheer unlined lace gave it a modernity and lightness that made it feel fresh and youthful,” Gillette tells TODAY.com.
Matos loves how the “exaggerated” hips of Kravitz’s dress “reworked a classic silhouette.”
“It felt cool, precise and entirely self-possessed,” says Matos.
Colman Domingo
Colman Domingo in Valentino stood out as one of the night’s best dressed men, says Matos.
The actor’s “richly colored, patterned Valentino look” felt “bold and graphic,” she says.
“The sharp tailoring and strong palette made it confident without feeling excessive,” adds Matos.
A$AP Rocky
Matos also admired how A$AP Rocky wore a “reworked Chanel look” with “classic tweed elements and layered styling.”
The rapper’s ensemble “felt effortless but considered, merging heritage codes with a modern edge,” she says.
Gracie Abrams
Gillette cited Gracie Abrams as one more star who nailed the Met Gala’s theme this year.
The “That’s So True” singer “absolutely glowed” in her Chanel gown, which was inspired by by a Gustav Klimt painting, says Gillette.
“With clear reference to the famous painter, it hit the fashion as art theme out of the park in the most beautiful way,” she adds.

title: See the Trailer for Season 2 of OWN’s ‘Heart & Hustle: Houston’
summary: Watch the trailer for Season 2 of of OWN’s "Heart & Hustle: Houston" and find out when the new season premieres. Plus, catch up with the cast.
story: “It’s all about sisterhood,” “Heart & Hustle: Houston” star Chloe Cook says in a trailer for the new season. But based on the footage, some friendships might be broken beyond repair.

The OWN reality television series, which follows a group of Black women looking for love and striving for success in Houston, will soon return for Season 2. This season, all the main cast members are back — Cook, La’Torria Lemon, LeBrina Jackson, Muneera Page, Roe Grady-Pichardo and Alaina Saulsberry — to face one other after the fallouts from Season 1.
TODAY.com is exclusively revealing the trailer for Season 2, which shows some of the ladies breaking off into separate groups as they struggle to rebuild their friendships.

In the teaser, the ladies are initially seen celebrating each other and exploring their romantic connections. Next, the clip takes a turn. “Life’s good, but behind the scenes…” Cook begins before the tone changes.

Saulsberry opens up about struggling to keep her family together. Elsewhere, Grady-Pichardo sheds tears as she worries about her children feeling like she is keeping them from their father. An emotional Lemon says, “I feel like my mom gave up. And not only did she give up on everything, she gave up on me.”
Page and Cook confront the men in their lives, with Cook wondering if she was cheated on.
However, the trailer hints that not everything is as it seems. “There are two sides to every story,” Jackson notes.
Some of the women are also not on speaking terms and one is called out for being a “mean girl.”

All the drama will unfold when Season 2 of “Heart & Hustle: Houston” premieres on OWN Saturday, May 30, at 9 p.m. ET/PT. New episodes will then air regularly at 8 p.m. ET/PT starting Saturday, June 20.
Watch the full clip and then get a refresher on everything you need to know about the cast, below.
‘Heart & Hustle: Houston’ Season 2 Cast
The six main cast members of “Heart & Hustle: Houston” are all reuniting for Season 2. Here’s what you need to remember about each member of the friend group and what to expect this season.

Cook is hoping to take the next step in her relationship with Jordan, but trust issues pop up before he can propose. Meanwhile, Cook’s family is skeptical about the couple’s 17-year age gap. As for her career, Cook leans into her passion for event planning and tries to rebuild her business.
Lemon is busy running her award-winning public relations firm and is also looking to expand her philanthropic impact. Privately, she is grieving the loss of her father and is looking to heal.

Jackson wants to further her progress in the wellness space as the owner of an elite spa. It seems she could be the “mean girl” mentioned in the trailer as her relationship with the other women becomes fractured.
Page is managing being a mom, wife and career woman. She and her husband, Norm, are struggling with their emotional connection and she hopes to repair their relationship.
Grady-Pichardo is an innovator and restaurateur that is involved in the Houston nightlife scene. Outside of work, she is a single mother who is co-parenting with her ex, Jayvon.
Saulsberry, the CEO of a makeup company, is comfortable in her work life after building a beauty empire. But outside of work, she and her husband, Josh, are separated and heading toward a divorce.

title: Whitney Leavitt Speaks Out About ‘SLOMW’ Exit and When She’s Leaving
summary: Whitney Leavitt is confirming she will exit “The Secret Lives of Mormon Lives” and when she plans to leave the Hulu reality TV series
story: Whitney Leavitt is confirming she will exit “The Secret Lives of Mormon Lives” and when she plans to leave the Hulu reality TV series.
The 32-year-old actor said in a May 5 selfie video on Instagram that the upcoming fifth season will be her last.

Leavitt’s update comes after she disclosed her exit from the show during her final performance in “Chicago” on Broadway on May 3, according to Deadline. Leavitt had been playing Roxie Hart since her debut in February.

“It’s honestly so crazy to me looking back on this journey because I had been trying to get into theater and film way before ‘Secret Lives’ even came into my life,” Leavitt said in her Instagram video.
She explained that “Secret Lives” fell into her life “organically.”
“It’s definitely not the path that I had envisioned in my mind to get to where I am today, but I wouldn’t change a thing,” she added.

The former MomTok influencer, who has been a cast member on the Hulu series since it premiered in September 2024, went on to say she “experienced so much” with the other women on the show and “learned so much” about herself.

“We have been through so much together, even more than what you’ve seen,” said Leavitt. “We’ve had really big highs, really big lows and no matter what happens with our relationships like that is something that will always be a part of our life, will always be a part of my life, and I wouldn’t change a thing.”
Leavitt concluded by saying she was leaving the reality series “with gratitude.”
“I feel content,” she said. “I feel like this is a chapter that’s closing in my life and honestly, I believe that’s how it was always meant to be.

“I’m so grateful for the ‘Secret Lives of Mormon Wives,’” she added. “It’s got me where I am today. It’s given me the opportunities that you all have seen but I’m ready. I’m ready for the next chapter and I can not wait to share with you guys what’s next.”

Leavitt’s starring role in “Chicago” proved to be wildly successful for the musical. She extended her role in March because of what the show’s producers called “an overwhelming demand” for tickets, People reported at the time. With Leavitt in as the show’s lead, “Chicago” broke records with the highest weekly ticket sales in the show’s 29-year history, according to People.

title: What’s It Like Inside the Met Gala? These Photos Give a Glimpse Behind the Scenes
summary: The Met Gala has long been considered fashion’s biggest night, and the 2026 event certainly lived up to those expectations
story: The Met Gala has long been considered fashion’s biggest night, and the 2026 event certainly lived up to those expectations.
On May 4, Hollywood A-listers stepped out in style to attend the annual charity fundraiser and fashion exhibition at New York’s Metropolitan Museum of Art.
Revisit the all top moments from the 2026 Met Gala

This year’s theme, “Costume Art,” featured a dress code described as “fashion is art.” Watching attendees walk the red carpet is always the highlight of the night for fashion lovers, especially since there’s a no-phones policy inside the event, per The Hollywood Reporter.
But just because celebrities can’t share a look inside, that doesn’t mean there aren’t photographers on hand.

Lucky for us, the professionals captured behind-the-scenes snapshots that revealed a few outfit changes and some fun celebrity pairings. Below, we’re sharing a few of the standout photos of the night.
Sabrina Carpenter's Outfit Changes
On the red carpet, Sabrina Carpenter sported a Dior gown that was made of cascading strips of film and accessorized with a jeweled headpiece.
Inside the event, the singer changed into a vibrant dress with a crisscross neckline and prints of Andy Warhol’s pop art of Marilyn Monroe.

While performing with Stevie Nicks, Carpenter slipped into yet another ensemble: a gold spaghetti strap mini dress with shimmery tassels. Meanwhile, Nicks also looked gorgeous in an embellished black gown.
Kendall Jenner's Wings
Kendall Jenner channeled her inner Greek goddess at the 2026 Met Gala in a Zac Posen gown that was inspired by “Winged Victory of Samothrace,” a statue in the Louvre museum in Paris.
While speaking with E! News’ La La Anthony, Posen said Jenner’s ensemble may include a “surprise” that would make its debut inside the event.
Photographers captured a photo of the stunning surprise: a set of wings that helped her channel the statue even more.
The Fashionable Co-Chairs
Alongside Anna Wintour, the 2026 Met Gala co-chairs included Nicole Kidman, Beyoncé and Venus Williams.
On the red carpet, Kidman opted for a red sequined Chanel gown as she posed for photos alongside daughter Sunday Rose, 17.
Meanwhile, Beyoncé walked the red carpet in an embellished Olivier Rousteing ensemble as she was accompanied by her 14-year-old daughter, Blue Ivy Carter, who made her Met Gala debut.
Williams also understood the assignment and showed up in a Swarovski crystal mesh gown, which she paired with a Wimbledon plate-inspired necklace.

Inside the event, two of the co-chairs changed their outfits. Kidman slipped into a red and cream gown with feathers and embellishments. Beyoncé brought the drama in a black long sleeve gown with sequins for days, a wide train and a veil she wore over her face.
Sza, Doechii and Hailey Bieber

Inside the event, attendees have the opportunity to catch up with old friends and mingle with some new faces. Photographers caught Sza, Doechii and Hailey Bieber together inside and snapped this stunning shot.

Per Vogue, Bieber was sporting a custom Saint Laurent dress with a 24-karat gold sculpted bodice. Doechii rocked a burgundy Marc Jacobs wrap dress and Sza opted for a yellow Bode dress with a larger-than-life headpiece.
Rihanna, Ciara and Katy Perry
One photographer captured this photo of three female powerhouse singers chatting inside the event.
Rihanna, who spoke with TODAY's Sheinelle Jones on the red carpet, wore a sculptural gold and silver metallic look from Maison Margiela FW25 Couture.

On the red carpet, Perry kept her identity under wraps at first while wearing a mesh and silver mask with her white off-the-shoulder Stella McCartney gown. Inside the event, she took the mask off to pose for a photo.
Ciara also dressed to impress in an Egyptian-inspired gold gown and headpiece.
The ‘Heated Rivalry’ Guys
"Heated Rivalry" stars Connor Storrie and Hudson William appeared to have a blast inside the event. In one photo, Storrie was seen dancing alongside Tate McRae and Blackpink singer Rosé.
Williams also mingled with a member of Blackpink, Lisa, at the event and showed off his keen fashion sense in a custom Balenciaga look.

title: Tina Knowles Says Beyonce’s Full Circle Moment at the Met Gala ‘Brought Tears to My Eyes’
summary: Beyoncé's mother, Tina Knowles, described how she felt watching her daughter return to the Met Gala, with granddaughter, Blue Ivy.
story: Beyoncé attended the Met Gala for the first time in a decade this year. It was an appearance that felt like déjà vu to her mother.

The “Halo” singer served as co-chair for the May 4 gala, which marked the first time she turned out for the event in 10 years. She was joined this year by husband Jay-Z and their daughter, Blue Ivy, 14.
“Oh, my God, I got really emotional. Shed a few tears,” Beyoncé’s mother, Tina Knowles, said when she appeared May 5 on the 3rd hour of TODAY.
Knowles said it was a full circle moment.

“It was kind of reminiscent, because Beyoncé started, you know, her first red carpet at 15, and Blue is 14. And so just seeing her out there looking confident and beautiful, it just brought tears to my eyes.”
Al Roker noted that Beyoncé and Blue “were like looking at both versions of the same (person) at the same time.”
“It’s like déjà vu,” Knowles added.
Knowles was also wowed by Sheinelle Jones dressing up as her daughter for TODAY’s Halloween spectacular last year.
“She was amazing,” Knowles said. “I posted it on my Instagram, and I couldn’t wait to show Beyoncé. She was so excited.”

Beyonce, who got a shoutout from Sheinelle at the Met Gala, has also spurred rumors that act three of her planned trilogy is coming any day now, even as TODAY.com has learned a release following 2023’s “Renaissance” and 2024’s “Cowboy Carter” is not imminent.
Don’t ask her mother, though, when to expect it.
“You know, I’ve said it before. I could tell you, but I’d have to kill you,” she joked.

title: Trump calls Iran’s response to U.S. peace proposal ‘totally unacceptable’
summary: President Donald Trump rejected Iran’s response to a U.S. proposal to bring an end to the war in the Middle East, calling the message “totally unacceptable” on Truth Social.“I have just read the response from Iran’s so-called ‘Representatives,’” Trump posted Sunday afternoon.
story: President Donald Trump rejected Iran’s response to a U.S. proposal to bring an end to the war in the Middle East, calling the message “totally unacceptable” on Truth Social.
Subscribe to read this story ad-free
Get unlimited access to ad-free articles and exclusive content.
“I have just read the response from Iran’s so-called ‘Representatives,’” Trump posted Sunday afternoon. “I don’t like it.”
Trump did not offer details about Iran’s response, which Iranian state media reported was sent through Pakistani mediators.

Talks between Tehran and Washington have stalled in recent weeks. The two sides continued to trade fire in the Persian Gulf on Saturday, more than a month after a temporary ceasefire deal that was initially intended to reopen the Strait of Hormuz was announced.
Iran has continued to block ships from passing through the strait, disrupting critical Gulf oil supplies and prolonging the global economic uncertainty surrounding the conflict.
Markets have soared and oil prices have dropped over the last week, however, amid anticipation that a deal could be close after weeks of talks and occasional setbacks.
Energy Secretary Chris Wright told NBC News’ “Meet the Press” on Sunday that the U.S. was still waiting for a “clear resolution” from Iran on its latest proposal.
He told moderator Kristen Welker that the U.S. was seeking the “free flow of traffic through the international waters that are the Straits of Hormuz, and an end to the Iranian nuclear program.”
“When we start to get free flow of traffic through the Strait of Hormuz, energy prices will come down,” he added.

Mike Waltz, the U.S. ambassador to the United Nations, meanwhile told ABC News that the negotiations had gone “longer and slower, I think, than anyone would like” but stressed that “those negotiations and that diplomacy is ongoing.”
Trump’s plan for military vessels to escort merchant ships through the Strait of Hormuz, known as “Project Freedom,” was paused last week just days after it was announced.
The U.S. has meanwhile continued its own blockade of Iran’s ports, but a U.S. intelligence analysis concluded Tehran could withstand a naval blockade for months.
In a phone interview with NBC News on Friday, Trump said “No” when asked if the conflict with Iran was over.
“It’s over when it’s over,” the president said. “But we certainly have won militarily. We have to get people to come out of the caves [in Iran] and sign something.”
Roughly 20% of the world’s oil passed through the Strait of Hormuz before the U.S. and Israel attacked Iran on Feb. 28.
In the interview, Trump said of the critical waterway: “We really control it; they don’t. And we’ve taken the business away from them.”
There are “no boats going into Iran,” he added. “They’re dying.”
More important than bringing about an immediate end to the conflict is striking a deal that is in the best interests of the U.S., he suggested.
“I don’t want to get there if it means I can’t make the best deal,” he said. “I’ve got to make good deals. We’re pretty close.”
Iran’s Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi said Friday that “every time a diplomatic solution is on the table, the U.S. opts for a reckless military adventure.”

The U.S. military blockade of Iran’s ports will eventually deprive Tehran of crucial oil revenue, but the regime could likely withstand the pressure for months without a major economic crisis or lasting damage to its oil fields, energy industry analysts and two Western officials familiar with intelligence assessments told NBC News.

Administration officials say the blockade is designed to cut off Iran’s oil exports — the country’s economic lifeline — and force Tehran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and bow to U.S. demands at the negotiating table.

Iran has gradually begun to cut back oil production due to the blockade and, at some point within the next two months, it may run out of storage capacity for its oil and even be forced to shut down some wells. But most analysts say Iran can probably avoid shutting down wells since a significant amount of its oil can be consumed domestically, allowing most oil fields to keep operating.

title: Americans evacuated from hantavirus-stricken cruise ship off Spanish island
summary: The cruise ship at the center of a deadly hantavirus outbreak arrived early Sunday off the Spanish island of Tenerife, where the process of sending most passengers back to their home countries will get underway.
story: TENERIFE, Spain — American passengers aboard the cruise ship at the center of a deadly hantavirus outbreak began boarding buses Sunday evening for evacuation flights out of Spain.
Subscribe to read this story ad-free
Get unlimited access to ad-free articles and exclusive content.

The 17 Americans aboard the MV Hondius are expected to be flown to the U.S. and monitored at the National Quarantine Unit on the campus of the University of Nebraska Medical Center, a specialized facility for highly hazardous communicable diseases. An 18th evacuee — a British national who lives in the U.S. — was among them.

After the Nebraska stop, the remaining passengers will be taken to one or more destinations so they can receive a clinical assessment and get “appropriate care and support based on their condition” at a Regional Emerging Special Pathogen Treatment Center, the Department of Health and Human Services said in a news release.

Passengers started evacuating the cruise ship Sunday shortly after it arrived in Tenerife, the largest of Spain’s Canary Islands off West Africa. The first group of passengers, wearing masks and head-to-toe personal protective equipment, were kept strictly secluded from members of the public as a small boat brought them ashore.

A medical tent was set up ready to receive passengers in what is expected to be a two-day operation at the island’s Granadilla port, with buses on hand to take them to the airport, where countries have arranged special flights to take them home.

The first plane carrying 14 Spanish passengers left Tenerife on Sunday for the Spanish capital, Madrid, where they were taken to Gómez Ulla Hospital, Spanish Health Minister Mónica García said. Planes carrying French, Canadian and British passengers were also preparing to depart Sunday evening. The passengers will be hospitalized for monitoring upon repatriation.

The four Canadian passengers will be taken to a predetermined location in British Columbia, where they will self-isolate for at least 21 or up to 42 days if there is a need to extend, the country’s public health officials said.

The five French passengers will be monitored at a hospital for three days and sent to quarantine at home for 45 days, according to France’s foreign ministry. One of the French passengers had developed symptoms, and the necessary protocols will be implemented, García said at a news conference Sunday.

Six confirmed cases of hantavirus and two suspected cases have been linked to the outbreak on the ship, the World Health Organization said Friday. Three of those people have died, officials said, including two who died aboard the ship.

The ship is anchored offshore in the Canary Islands to enable the evacuation after Spanish officials overruled local leaders who had opposed the move, fearful of the infection risk and any potential economic hit to Tenerife’s tourism-dominated economy.
“The risk to the public is low,” WHO Director-General Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus told reporters after the first plane departed. “So they shouldn’t be scared, and they shouldn’t panic.”
“Based on scientific assessment and based on evidence, the risk is low,” he added.
In their home countries, many will be taken to isolation facilities.
The WHO is recommending a long quarantine period. Diana Rojas, the head of high-impact diseases, said Sunday that “we cannot be sure that they will not develop symptoms until 42 days have passed.”

Garcia said passengers from the Netherlands were the next group to leave the vessel after Spanish citizens, with their plane also carrying German, Belgian and Greek passengers, as well as part of the crew.

Passengers from Turkey, France, ⁠the U.K. and the U.S. were then expected to be evacuated, followed by six people from “Australia, New Zealand and Asia,” she said, as part of the last flight planned for Monday.
Dr. Boris Pavlin, a WHO medical epidemiologist, told NBC News the operation had been “extremely efficient.”

Passengers have been coming off the ship on boats “in small numbers, placed on buses and spaced apart, just to make sure that — even though all of them are asymptomatic, they have no symptoms right now — that they don’t present any additional new risk to each other,” he said.

“This is not Covid,” he added. “In Covid, we’ve all been traumatized by how people you didn’t even think were sick were already spreading it, [but] we have no reason to believe that that’s happening here.”
Add NBC News to Google
Add NBC News to Google

The Dutch-owned ship, along with some crewmembers and the passengers’ luggage, will continue on the five-day journey to Rotterdam in the Netherlands, according to cruise operator Oceanwide Expeditions.
The body of a person who died on board will also remain on the ship, which will undergo a disinfection process in the Netherlands, García said.
Health officials have stressed that the risk to the global population and to the residents of Tenerife, off western Africa, is low.

Hantavirus is typically contracted through contact with rodents, especially when people are exposed to their urine, droppings and saliva. The origins of the first case “suggest possible exposure to rodents during bird watching activities,” the WHO said.
Of the group of viruses, only the Andes — the strain in the Hondius case — is known to spread between people, but those people usually have very close contact with each other, according to the WHO.
On May 2, a month after the ship left Ushuaia, Argentina, “a cluster of passengers with severe respiratory illness” on board was reported to the WHO, the health organization said.
At that time, the ship had 147 passengers and crewmembers, but 34 passengers and crewmembers had previously disembarked, the WHO said.

The report came weeks after the first death, that of a Dutch man who died on board on April 11. At that point, “the cause of death was unknown and there was no evidence of a virus or contagion on board,” Oceanwide Expeditions has said.
His wife died at a South African clinic on April 26, the WHO said.
The third death, that of a German woman, happened on board on May 2, according to the WHO and Oceanwide Expeditions.
Two days later, hantavirus was confirmed in a passenger who had been medically evacuated to a hospital in South Africa, the company said.
Hantavirus can have a fatality rate of around 40% to 50%, the WHO says, and the elderly are particularly at risk. The average age of those aboard the ship is 65, it said.
Phil Helsel reported from Los Angeles, Freddie Clayton from London, Mirna Alsharif from New York and Mo Abbas and Daniele Hamamdjian from Tenerife.

title: Energy secretary says White House is open to suspending gas tax amid soaring prices
summary: Energy Secretary Chris Wright told NBC News’ “Meet the Press” on Sunday that the Trump administration is “open to all ideas” to lower the cost of gas, including suspending the federal gas tax
story: Energy Secretary Chris Wright told NBC News’ “Meet the Press” on Sunday that the Trump administration is “open to all ideas” to lower the cost of gas, including suspending the federal gas tax.
Subscribe to read this story ad-free
Get unlimited access to ad-free articles and exclusive content.
Gas prices have been soaring, hitting a national average of $4.52 per gallon on Sunday, according to data from motor club AAA — an increase of more than 50% since the start of the Iran war.

Asked by moderator Kristen Welker whether he would support suspending the federal gas tax, Wright said that “all measures that can be taken to lower the price at the pump and lower the prices for Americans, this administration is in support of.”
Asked to clarify whether President Donald Trump is open to suspending the federal gas tax, which stands at about 18 cents a gallon, Wright said, “We’re open to all ideas.”
“Everything has trade-offs,” he added.
Democrats in Congress introduced a bill in March to temporarily suspend the federal gas tax until October.
Wright declined to speculate when asked whether average gas prices, which remain a potent political issue during a midterm election year, could reach $5 per gallon.

“I can’t predict the price of energy in the short term or even the medium term, but what we’re doing is ending a 47-year conflict Iran has fought,” he said. “Death to the United States for 47 years, death to Israel.”
Pressed again on the possibility of gas prices hitting the $5 milestone, Wright said he was “just avoiding price predictions.”
“But I will say, the United States is in a tremendous position,” he added, pointing to U.S. production of oil and natural gas.

Wright previously told “Meet the Press” in March that there was a “very good chance” gas prices could drop below $3 per gallon by the summer. At the time, Wright predicted that “Americans will feel it for a few more weeks.” Eight weeks later, gas prices have continued to climb and shown no immediate sign of returning to prewar prices amid Iran’s continued blockade of the Strait of Hormuz.
Experts say that while the United States’ own blockade of Iranian ports would ultimately deprive Iran of necessary oil revenue, the country could likely withstand that pressure for months.
Last week, Trump launched an operation aimed at guiding ships through the Strait of Hormuz to break Iran’s hold on the crucial waterway, but he ultimately paused it.
In a separate interview on CBS News’ “Face the Nation,” Wright was asked whether the U.S. would resume “Project Freedom” anytime soon.

“If it’s clear in the next few days that there’s not a good path to a negotiated settlement, we’ll go back to the military method to open the strait,” he said. “The economic pressure on Iran right now is increasing dramatically.”

title: It started as a joke. Now India’s Diet Coke parties are all the rage as shortages hit shelves.
summary: Love of Diet Coke runs deep for Ishika Gupta, but it was the fear of losing her favorite drink that took the obsession to new heights
story: Love of Diet Coke runs deep for Ishika Gupta, but it was the fear of losing her favorite drink that took the obsession to new heights.
Subscribe to read this story ad-free
Get unlimited access to ad-free articles and exclusive content.

The closure of the Strait of Hormuz is still rippling across the global economy, and disruption has been particularly acute in parts of Asia, touching everything from gas and energy costs to cooking fuel and fertilizers.
But the shipping logjam in the Persian Gulf is also having unexpected impacts. In India, shops have struggled to keep Diet Coke on the shelves due to a shortage of aluminum cans.

Suppliers told the Reuters news agency that some orders were not being fulfilled due to a can shortage caused by the situation in the Gulf, which accounts for around 9% of global aluminum production. Diet Coke is not sold in plastic bottles in India, unlike most other countries, leaving fans of the drink at risk of losing out.
For Gupta, a 25-year-old marketing and design consultant based in New Delhi, it was an opportunity for fun, so she decided to throw a party celebrating the drink.
“It was a joke,” said Gupta, describing herself as an “avid drinker” of Diet Coke. “I thought only me and two of my friends would show up.”

title: Map: See how gas prices have changed in the county where you live
summary: How much gas prices have increased during the Iran war depends entirely on where you live, an NBC News analysis of average gas price data from AAA shows.
story: Gas prices are up across the country, but nowhere are they up as much as in northern Kentucky’s Kenton County, where an average gallon of gas would run you $4.92 a gallon Tuesday, nearly double the price it was at the start of the Iran war.
Subscribe to read this story ad-free
Get unlimited access to ad-free articles and exclusive content.

How much gas prices have increased depends entirely on where you live, an NBC News analysis of average gas price data from AAA shows. Kenton County prices were up $2.32 a gallon, while Fisher County in Texas, population 3,692, prices had only increased 60 cents as of Tuesday.

It also depends on the state. In Georgia, gas prices are up an average of $1.16 per gallon, the lowest state-level increase in the nation. But in Ohio, the average price is up $2.06 a gallon, the largest state-level increase in the U.S.
This map, showing the latest gas prices and the change in prices since the start of the war, will be updated daily.

title: Birders push back on hantavirus fears tied to Argentine city
summary: The hantavirus outbreak on a cruise that departed from Argentina last month has cast an unwelcome spotlight on Tierra del Fuego, a region well known for its biodiversity and bird-watching, authorities and guides say
story: The hantavirus outbreak on a cruise that departed from Argentina last month has cast an unwelcome spotlight on Tierra del Fuego, a region well known for its biodiversity and bird-watching, authorities and guides say.
Subscribe to read this story ad-free
Get unlimited access to ad-free articles and exclusive content.

Now, bird-watching may be at the epicenter of the outbreak on board the MV Hondius, in which three people have died and five others were sickened. The World Health Organization has said the first person with a confirmed case may have been exposed to rodents — which can carry hantavirus — while on a birding trip.
The farthest stretches of the South American continent are a strong draw for bird-watchers, National Geographic author and birding expert Noah Strycker said.

“South America has more bird species than any other continent, and Argentina is a superb destination for birders,” he said by email. “Ushuaia is surrounded by beautiful beech forests and snow-capped mountains, with Tierra del Fuego National Park just outside the city. Birders hope to find Magellanic Woodpeckers and White-bellied Seedsnipes there, among other specialties.”

title: 17 American passengers aboard hantavirus-hit cruise ship will quarantine in Nebraska
summary: Seventeen American passengers aboard the cruise ship at the center of a hantavirus outbreak will quarantine at a Nebraska facility that specializes in handling patients with highly communicable diseases, health officials said
story: Seventeen American passengers aboard the cruise ship at the center of a hantavirus outbreak will quarantine at a Nebraska facility that specializes in handling patients with highly communicable diseases, health officials said.
Subscribe to read this story ad-free
Get unlimited access to ad-free articles and exclusive content.
The MV Hondius is expected to arrive in Tenerife in Spain’s Canary Islands on Sunday.

A team from the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention will meet the American passengers there, an agency official confirmed to NBC News. And the State Department is arranging a repatriation flight back to the U.S. for them, a department spokesperson said Friday.

The 17 passengers will be received at the National Quarantine Unit, a secured facility on the University of Nebraska Medical Center campus in Omaha, said Dr. Michael Wadman, the unit’s medical director.
There, teams will assess them and determine any necessary quarantine measures, he told reporters Friday. They will also be monitored daily.

All of the people being transported to Nebraska are in good health and are asymptomatic, but should anyone be diagnosed with the virus, they would would be moved to the Nebraska Biocontainment Unit, said Dr. Angela Hewlett, the medical director of that unit.

The Biocontainment Unit treats patients with hazardous communicable diseases in sterile environments that maximize safety and containment. It features an isolation unit, a HEPA filtration system and specialized sterilization “autoclaves” with double doors to decontaminate waste and linens.
Hewlett emphasized that the current outbreak is not at Covid levels.
“I do not see this as progressing to a worldwide pandemic, although there’s still a lot of unknowns, and I think we all need to recognize that,” she said.
Six confirmed cases
Six cases of the Andes hantavirus have been confirmed and two cases are probable, the World Health Organization said Friday.

Two people with confirmed cases and one person with a probable case have died, it said, as countries around the world seek to trace and monitor passengers who left the Hondius at points along its voyage.
Cruise operator Oceanwide Expeditions said Friday that none of the passengers who remain aboard the Hondius have tested positive for hantavirus or are showing symptoms.
Flight passenger being treated
Spanish officials said a woman from Alicante who was on the same flight as a passenger who later died of hantavirus is being treated at a hospital for a suspected infection.

The Spanish Health Ministry said in a Friday briefing that a 32-year-old woman was being treated in her home city after the regional Valencian health authority contacted her through a Europe-wide health alert system.

The woman was “sitting two rows behind the person who died from hantavirus, a person who tested positive, having had only brief contact as they were on board the plane for a short time,” Secretary of State for Health Javier Padilla told a news conference.
On Saturday the woman’s PCR test came back negative for hantavirus, Spanish Minister of Health Mónica Garcia said. A second diagnostic test will be carried out after 24 hours, she said.

The unidentified Dutch woman who later died — whose husband died at sea aboard the Hondius — was removed from a flight from Johannesburg to Amsterdam because staff were so concerned about her condition.
On Friday, the WHO confirmed that a flight attendant, who was not on the ship, was negative for the virus following testing at a hospital in the Netherlands.
A German national has also died in the outbreak.
Add NBC News to Google
Add NBC News to Google
Suspected case on Atlantic island

On Friday, British authorities confirmed a new suspected case on the tiny Atlantic island of Tristan da Cunha, one of the most remote inhabited places on Earth, with about 220 permanent residents, where the Hondius made its first scheduled stop on April 15.
Stephen Doughty, the U.K. minister for overseas territories, wrote in a letter to the Tristan da Cunha administration that the patient was “an islander” and that their spouse was isolating.
Doughty added that four islanders hitched a lift with the Hondius to nearby St. Helena.

The U.K. Health Security Agency confirmed that this patient was on board the Hondius and added that two additional British nationals have now been confirmed to have the virus, which is typically contracted through contact with rodents. Person-to-person transmission is rare, though officials say it is possible through close personal contact.
Disembarkation plans

The Hondius is making its way from Cape Verde, in western Africa, to the Canary Islands ahead of its planned docking at Tenerife, when the WHO, alongside Dutch, British and Spanish officials, will also collaborate in the evacuation and testing of the remaining passengers.

The Spanish Health Ministry released its protocol plan on Friday for those disembarking from the Hondius. The plan will require passengers and staff who were aboard the ship between April 1 and May 10 — or who had close contact with a confirmed case — to quarantine at a Madrid military hospital under active monitoring.

“Passengers will remain in single rooms without visitors,” the health ministry said in a news release. “During this period, they will undergo a PCR test upon arrival and another seven days later. Active surveillance will also be conducted, including twice-daily temperature checks to detect any compatible symptoms early.”

If a patient develops a fever, shortness of breath or other relevant symptoms, they will be transferred to a negative-pressure isolation room, the health ministry said. If they test positive, the patient “will be admitted to a High-Level Isolation and Treatment Unit (UATAN) until clinical recovery.”
The U.K. health agency said all British passengers will be asked to isolate for 45 days upon returning to the U.K., noting that the disease’s incubation period can be up to six weeks.

The U.S. State Department — which is arranging flights for the Americans in coordination with the CDC, the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services and the Spanish government — is prepared to provide consular assistance as soon as the ship arrives in Tenerife, a spokesperson said.
The spokesperson added that the State Department is in “direct communication” with Americans aboard the ship.
In Tenerife, some residents are anxious about the disembarkation and what it would mean for the island and its tourism industry if hantavirus were to spread there.

The WHO and health officials in numerous countries have stressed that the risk of the outbreak spreading to wider populations is low and that hantavirus is only transmitted through close personal contact, unlike airborne diseases such as Covid-19.
Global investigation
A WHO investigation is underway to identify the source of the hantavirus outbreak, which is rare but endemic in parts of Argentina where the Hondius began its voyage.

The WHO said Friday that the “working hypothesis” is that the first case — the man who died on the ship 10 days after boarding on April 1 — was infected at some point during his activities in Argentina and Chile.
The man had spent three months traveling through Argentina, Chile and Uruguay before boarding, the WHO said. His is considered a probable case, the WHO said.
Oceanwide Expeditions said Friday that the mood on board the vessel remained calm, with guests and crew following procedures and under the guidance of a medical team.

Khabir Moraes, a chef working on the Hondius, praised the medical response in an Instagram post. He assured his followers that he is “fit, fine, and doing well” and said there were no additional hantavirus cases.

“The situation on board is being managed with the utmost care,” he wrote in the post. “We are in excellent hands, with three additional doctors now on board to assist us, including a representative from the World Health Organization.”
The cruise operator said Friday that one of the people evacuated from the ship, who had shown potential signs of hantavirus, was the ship’s doctor.
At least seven Americans who were on the vessel are isolating at home across five states, and none show any symptoms of the virus, according to local health officials.

title: Spanish island braces for hantavirus cruise ship as WHO urges calm amid protests
summary: The head of the World Health Organization made an appeal to locals in Tenerife to remain calm it prepared to receive passengers from a hantavirus-hit cruise ship that has left three people dead and sparked growing local outrage.
story: TENERIFE, Spain — The head of the World Health Organization on Saturday made an appeal to locals in Tenerife to remain calm as the Spanish island prepared to receive passengers from a hantavirus-hit cruise ship that has left three people dead and sparked growing local outrage.
Subscribe to read this story ad-free
Get unlimited access to ad-free articles and exclusive content.

Residents have taken to the streets in recent days to protest the impending arrival of the MV Hondius to the Canary Islands after the Spanish government overruled local leaders to grant permission for the ship to anchor offshore Sunday. Many on the tourism-reliant island worry that even a carefully coordinated disembarkation could harm the region’s image and economy.
Protests continued Friday, with residents chanting, “Yes to tourism, no to the virus.”
Eight cases of hantavirus have been linked to the outbreak aboard the ship, according to the latest WHO update Saturday, and three people have died.
WHO Director-General Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus issued a direct appeal to locals Saturday, reassuring them that Sunday’s operation posed little risk to the public.
Though the virus is “serious,” he said, “the risk to you, living your daily life in Tenerife, is low. This is the WHO’s assessment, and we do not make it lightly.”
He stressed that the WHO asked Spain to accept the ship under a “legally binding framework,” adding that nearly 150 passengers and crew from 23 countries have been at sea for weeks.

“Some of them grieving, all of them frightened, all of them longing for home,” Tedros said. “Tenerife has been chosen because it has the medical capacity, the infrastructure and the humanity to help them reach safety.”

The ship is expected to anchor offshore early Sunday. Most of the passengers and crew members will be ferried ashore at the Granadilla port in small boats and taken directly to planes to repatriation flights in sealed, guarded vehicles, Tedros said.
“You will not encounter them,” he assured residents. “Your families will not encounter them.”

Spanish citizens are expected to disembark first, according to Spanish Health Minister Mónica Garcia, who said at a news briefing Saturday evening that "everything is ready" for the arrival of the ship. All passengers will wear FFP2 masks, she added.
Tedros also said he would travel to Tenerife to “observe this operation firsthand, to stand alongside the health workers, port staff and officials who are making it happen.”

Dr. Maria Van Kerkhove, an infectious disease epidemiologist with the WHO, told the media in a briefing Saturday that health screenings aboard the ship continue and that anyone showing symptoms will be taken to the Netherlands for treatment. Passengers and crew members without symptoms will be flow to their home countries, she said.

No one currently aboard the ship has any symptoms, and contact tracing is underway to determine those who were likely to have been exposed. Everyone on the vessel is still considered a “high-risk contact,” Van Kerkhove said.
“The goal” is for all of the repatriation flights to take place Sunday and Monday, Van Kerkhove said.

Several nations, including the U.S., Germany, France, Belgium, Ireland and the Netherlands, have sent planes to evacuate their citizens, Spanish Interior Minister Fernando Grande-Marlaska said Saturday.
Thirty crew members will stay aboard the ship and sail to the Netherlands for the disinfection process, along with the body of one of the passengers who died.

The U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention has deployed a team to Tenerife to meet the 17 Americans who are due to disembark from the ship. They will be flown on a flight arranged by the State Department to a specialist quarantine facility in Nebraska previously used to house patients in the early days of the Covid-19 pandemic.
Their quarantine period is unclear. Dr. Michael Wadman, director of the National Quarantine Unit, has said the duration will be determined after an “epidemiological assessment.”
Van Kerkhove said Saturday that the WHO is recommending “active monitoring and follow-up” for all passengers and crew members for 42 days from their “last point of exposure” to confirmed cases.
Seventeen U.S. citizens are aboard the cruise ship, and seven others have already returned to the U.S., a CDC official said Saturday. All are being tracked by the CDC, the official said.

The Americans aboard the ship will be taken to Nebraska for monitoring and assessment, but they will not be placed under quarantine, the official said. He did not specify how long they will be kept in Nebraska but said the hope is that it will be a short stay.
The overall monitoring period will last 42 days and not necessarily take place entirely in Nebraska, as it will include self-monitoring after travelers return home, according to the official.

A U.S. Department of Health and Human Services official who was also at the briefing said that the risk to the American public remains extremely low and that there is no indication of increased risk to routine travel.
Daniele Hamamdjian and Mo Abbas reported from Tenerife, Mithil Aggarwal from London and Mirna Alsharif from New York City.

title: More than 100 sickened in norovirus outbreak aboard Caribbean Princess cruise
summary: Even after more than 100 passengers and crewmembers fell sick during a norovirus outbreak aboard the Caribbean Princess cruise ship, passenger Jan van Milligen said life on board still felt surprisingly normal.“The normal atmosphere is still here,” van Milligen, who has spent the last 21 days aboard the ship with relatives and friends from South Africa, told NBC News on Saturday.
story: Even after more than 100 passengers and crewmembers fell sick during a norovirus outbreak aboard the Caribbean Princess cruise ship, passenger Jan van Milligen said life on board still felt surprisingly normal.
Subscribe to read this story ad-free
Get unlimited access to ad-free articles and exclusive content.

“The normal atmosphere is still here,” van Milligen, who has spent the last 21 days aboard the ship with relatives and friends from South Africa, told NBC News on Saturday. “We went to a show last night, had dinner and breakfast this morning.”

The outbreak sickened 102 of the ship’s 3,116 passengers and 13 crewmembers, according to the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention. The predominant symptoms were vomiting and diarrhea, and the agency cited norovirus as the cause.
The CDC said the outbreak was reported Thursday during the ship’s April 28 to May 11 voyage through the Caribbean.
As of Saturday afternoon, the vessel was located north of the Dominican Republic en route to Nassau in the Bahamas, according to CruiseMapper.
